meeting.title: FW: Product X AppDev Office Hours

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: Teams keeps not automating with me when I join meetings. That's very annoying.

content.speaker: Jennifer
content.text: I actually kind of like this. I want to hear that's home.

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text:
Well, luckily, my husband... I don't know if it's lucky or not, it's slow season for a back right now, so every day I'm like, "Are you working today or not?" So right now, he's not working, so he's managing the kids.
So, screaming in the background, they are starting.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Yes.

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: So my husband...

content.speaker: Jennifer
content.text: In the back. So [Laughter] that's exciting.

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: Yes, I am very excited.

content.speaker: Jennifer
content.text:
Willie, this meeting... Let me just introduce you to this meeting. It's just kind of office hours for product. We either have some demos. If people have anything done before they push it out into production, this is where they demo it to our product owners.
Then sometimes we refine stories in here if we have questions for the product owners or for them to introduce different features to us. This is one of the best places so that we have less planned meetings throughout the day, saving everybody's time.
So it's not... Everyone's going to be in here today. I think we have one demo it sounds like from the other teams. So you'll actually get to see their new e-commerce solution that they're pushing out soon, and you'll see some of the things with that demo that you know Elvis will be presenting until he gets here. Beth and Wesley, do you guys want to introduce yourselves to Willie?

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: Sure.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Absolutely.

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text:
I'm Beth. I am the product manager over our sales vertical. So the e-commerce project that we're pushing out right now, that is what I am very closely working on. And following... I've been here for...
I think I'm coming up on two years in a month or so. And jumping everywhere right now. Help out with the legacy stuff if needed. Customer service support and all that kind of stuff. So if you have questions around any of the business processes or why we do what we do, I am a good person to ask.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: I am not the best person to ask because Beth is still teaching me along the way.

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: He...

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
Wesley Dawson, I am relatively new. I started back in mid-December. Actually, you've never seen my face.
Maybe that's a good intro as well. Good morning, Wesley Dawson. I am part of the Zolio team, we are part of the implementation team as part of Mandalor. My responsibilities... I more managed a team. So facilitating moving tickets through partnering with the team, understanding Beth's requirements, Beth's needs, the overall project goals, and spiriting the team through tour to align with that. I primarily worked with Beth, but generally, anything having to do with implementation tasks for the team Mandalor, please come to me. Let's have a conversation to see how we can pair together.

content.speaker: Jennifer
content.text:
Awesome. Now Doug and you, Elvis, just topped on. So Willie is our new senior developer. He's going to be on the indoor team right now. West just introduced himself to him since the Ovis and Douglas hopped on. Do you guys mind introducing yourselves and telling what you guys do here?
All this... I guess you already talked to Willie, so maybe we could skip you. But Doug, do you want to...?

content.speaker: Speaker 4
content.text: Sorry, I'll introduce myself. Hey, I'm Doug Wals. I'm on the product team, newer on the product team, focused on product growth, has been at Lifeline for gosh, almost five years now. I originally started on the marketing team, so I still have a heavy hand with everything related to the website, emails, and text messages.

content.speaker: Jennifer
content.text: And then. Willie, do you to just stay. Hi. And just give like a sentence or two of who you are for this group.

content.speaker: Speaker 5
content.text: So my name's Willie, and I've been a software engineer for over a decade, and I studied today. I'm excited to join the company. Awesome.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: Hi, Willie. Nice to have you aboard. I am the principal software engineer, so we have really met at the interviews. You did very well, so I'm glad to have you on the team and excited to start working together.

content.speaker: Speaker 5
content.text: Yeah, thanks.

content.speaker: Jennifer
content.text: Awesome. I think that's all of the introductions, so we are good to get started. If you have your demo, you us.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text:
I've been making a ton of changes, but mostly cosmetic, but some of them are more important like changes in the state of the application state management to simplify some of the things that we are doing.
Pretty much in the appointments, I am trying to follow more the design. I'm trying to avoid some of the issues that we had, especially, for example, this calendar. Now it's more like the design, it's using the right font sizes and everything. Previously, it was huge in a way that it was expanding over the phone limits. When you open it on the phone, especially now, it's more like following the design idea and not that huge is still good enough for clicking on it, but it's not like expanding, you know, to overloading the page.
But more than that, this section here we have some error messages I show you this yesterday and something else I wanted to review with you is like when we get to this page, let me refresh the cache. I was just by default, opening the browser location.
And. But you know, in most of the cases, the users are going to come here from a CD a and it's gonna come with the CIP code in the UR L. So that logic was over complicated. And I say, okay, maybe we can just get rid of that auto completion for the current, date.
If the user wants to use the location, they can click this button. But I want to know what? What do you think about? That?

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: Is it...?

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text:
Can you let me show you? Here, just a second. [Laughter] My brain is catching up. Okay, yeah, I know. Here, if you go to this page in most cases, all the cases, I expect the user to come here with a zip code right in the URL or something like that because they are coming from the live line screening homepage.
So here you have to select the zip code and then it's going to open that with that particular zip code. So that is working. You can compare with the SIP code and it's working. The only thing I removed is the ability to... In case the user is just going straight to an appointment, we are completing this information with the user's browser location like "This is my address."
So I removed that because that's overcomplication in the code and it's just basically if the user is coming here for the first time and they are not coming with the ST call, they can just hit this button. I don't know.

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: Right. Yeah. If they... We shouldn't be autograbbing their location. I think they have to click that.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: Yeah, I mean most of the cases, as I say, they are coming with the code so that they will have something here already.

content.speaker: Stace
content.text:
Yeah, I think at least launching with... And... Thoughts on this, but the problem with trying to autograb it is most of the time you get trapped behind a permission. Yeah. Thing, right. Which kind of locks up your screen and everything.
But, for you... I do want to just keep in mind that the normal traffic pattern is not organic search from the home page. Most users are going to land here from a digital ad source, right? Meta... Most users are going to get here from Meta.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: Yeah, but they are they're gonna go to okay, go ahead.

content.speaker: Speaker 4
content.text:
I was just going to say, what we're kind of promoting for a lot of the landing page traffic is because remember, like this start page, the Heart Health is was one of our Primary Landing Page Designs and then brought over here and that's the entry point so that all this content exists for people landing from paid ads.
Well, since this content won't exist at the start of the new experience, what we're working on is building specific landing pages on the new website that will route in kind of like we did the e com. So a lot of those people will come from an experience somewhere else in the domain with the zip code.
So that actually thing that Elvis just demo is going to probably happen, I think a lot more than it ever has got every.

content.speaker: Stace
content.text: That's good to know. Just for future reference then if we go back to that crossing sub domains got to remember what might get lost for tracking purposes.

content.speaker: Speaker 4
content.text: Yeah. Is there not some kind of, I guess trust that can exist between WW can.

content.speaker: Stace
content.text: But we have to find that right by. Yeah, by default, browsers don't obey that. So when we use local storage or create cookies or things like that, it has to be start to all subdimains.

content.speaker: Speaker 4
content.text: I think we had the. The unbalanced domain. The discovered dot and I'm. I'm pushing for them to not use that anymore. Once we switched to book dot and only use www for landing pages. Okay.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text:
So basically, that's my understanding. The user is never coming straight to this appointment page. They are going to go through a home page. It could be either this one or a different one that we implement.
But then here they would have more information about everything, and they can have the decision to start the process. They usually put the... And we start the process from there. But if that changes, I can adjust the PRI. Here I did a few other fixes. As you can see, these three remaining are hard core values. We will have here more than three. I just removed the three remaining. I just said, "Show more," because I don't have the information of that number coming from the API, so it's just "Show more." You can load more locations. I did a bunch of follow-up fixes, but...
Okay. Let me see. In case the user refreshes the page, at this point, everything is preserved. My concern with this is that in case the user wants to refresh the appointments for whatever reason, that's not going to happen.
Okay, if they hit refresh on the browser, we are not triggering a new search. Is that okay?

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: Is there not a reset in the Figma? Did we miss that in the design?

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: I haven't seen that.

content.speaker: Stace
content.text: Okay, I think that's... I think it's okay, and probably better, right? It's not a very dynamic search, so chances are you're going to get the same thing back anyway.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text:
Yeah. So I would say I don't like the way we are doing this, but it's because of the legacy. But you know, I showed a stat on Greg. Some other approaches that we can follow in the future, and this will be a super good experience.
But for now, we are dealing with that legacy system, and this is not gray. There are a lot of issues that when the user could have selected an appointment, another user could have selected the same appointment as well. In those cases, we will have to deal with those issues. Other than that...
Okay, so now I add this border here when you select something. You will see that border is in the design and a few other fixes here and there. I just made this four calls, but I noticed now that it's sometimes more... I need to probably look into that, but that's a very small detail.
A lot of CSS improvements like phone sizing and everything is aligned to the design, not in this step, but in multiple steps in packages. For example, this included is like the design. It's not the one you see here that was like the alignment was not gray.
This section here is like the design as well. You can see it's like the design. It's not like this that was not that great.
The ready buttons are more like the design as well. Yeah, they were using this checkmark, but now the ready buttons are easier to hit and see.
Yeah. The main changes are basically in this area of
appointments and everything else. When you select this, it's going to go back to the appointment and it's going to...
Okay. He used to... But it was refreshing a new search. I need to fix that detail. But yeah, I made multiple adjustments, but I just wanted to double-share with you those that were the most important ones that now... Here in this behavior, you refresh. We were like removing everything and that was probably okay, but now I am moving everything to systems because it's way easier to maintain the code and the experience is probably even better because it's faster and everything
is preserved. We are preserving the user selection. What's going on with this system is the information here, the selection where in local storage.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Could you say that one more time, please? You're moving everything to WA.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text:
So you make a selection here, like this, for example, and then you refresh or whatever. You will lose that selection, but now it's not losing anything.
Do you know something I noticed here? Every time we switch the legs, this is breaking. There is something broken with one of the legs, for sure.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: So you have a... We have a ticket to investigate the appointment route. Not working, are you? Is your hypothesis that it's related to a leg switch?

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text:
Yeah, because in my local, that's working fine. It was working fine yesterday, probably in the afternoon. So I think it's something with the leg, maybe, but in my local, that's working.
Maybe when I made my changes, that would work. I don't know if I fixed it that way. But I don't think that should be the case. But I don't know, it's definitely not working.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
Can you just with the ticket that you have for appointment, can you just do a little bit of investigation and maybe I can help you as well?
I'm not sure it's a leg switch, but if it is indeed a leg switch, I need to understand the root cause of that ASAP.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: Yeah, that's concerning. Yeah, I dont know why and why this is specifically are the appointment is like four or four and breaking. Okay. What else I had for today?

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: PA magic.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text:
I think I had something.
Okay. I wanted to confirm, this ticket the...
We are not moving forward with this ticket the reducing the account IDI did PLC an implementation of this approach. But the thing is, if we want to reduce the account IDs, the idea here is just to try to avoid the orphan parent accounts that we are adding to recording.
We had the discussion. I think... Stays and Beth that it's okay to have those parent accounts to be disabled on recording for the failing attempt to complete the purchase rather than if we vote this way, we need to keep those accounts active while the user is retrying.
But in some cases, the user may not finish the purchase, and in those cases, those accounts are going to stay active if we go this way. That's probably worse than having been disabled.

content.speaker: Stace
content.text: Let's put this back in the backlog instead of totally closing it and let's assess to see what happens when we're live. More confusion here, right? Because the parent-child account again is not the happy path. That only happens for a very small number of sales.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: Yeah. I created this branch with the implementation. So in case we want to go with that, we can just review it.

content.speaker: Stace
content.text: Yeah, I'm fine with launching and seeing what happens. If it becomes a mess, we'll do something. If it's fine...

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: What's a variable status?

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Put it on hold, please. It's still on the board. We'll just know that not to give it too much attention in stand-ups.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text:
Okay.
No, that's it. What I want you to do is I'm going to create a pull request. I'm going to document the changes, but I would like E Wesley or Beth to help me review it in the PR environment before I merge.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: For your larger for the larger refactor, right? That's what I'm referring to.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: Yes.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Yeah, I'm with you. We can play out scenarios. Maybe I can do it on mobile and/or I can do it on desktop and Chrome versus Safari. We can play those out.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: Yeah. Let me know if you find...

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: Risk of merging it.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text:
The reason is I am doing significant changes in the state management and everything else. I would rather make adjustments in the pull request rather than creating an additional ticket for those fixes.
It's faster for me, and when you give me feedback, I can just incorporate it immediately. I don't need to wait for another day for...

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text: So just because my bandwidth is very limited. If it's a matter of QA, then we should have Devon running through all the test scenarios that he has. Then if there's anything outstanding from there, then I can help you guys with any direction that's still needed.

content.speaker: Yoelvis
content.text: Yeah.

content.speaker: bethany.duffy@llsa.com
content.text:
Sounds good.
Alright. Anything else for this?
Cool. Thanks, everyone.
